title: Understanding Angular Motion
text: To find the final angular velocity, we need to use the kinematic equation for rotational motion. This is similar to the equations used in linear motion but adapted for rotation.

title: Identify the Known Values
text:
We know the following:
- Initial angular velocity (ω₀) $$=$$ $$0$$ $$ rac{rad}{s}$$ (since it starts from rest)
- Angular displacement (θ) $$=$$ $$300$$ rad
- Angular acceleration (\alpha) $$=$$ $$5.0$$ rad/s²

title: Choose the Right Equation
text: The equation that relates angular displacement, initial angular velocity, angular acceleration, and final angular velocity is:\nω² $$=$$ ω₀² + 2\alphaθ

title: Substitute the Known Values
text: Substitute the known values into the equation:\nω² $$=$$ 0² + $$2\times5\times300$$

title: Solve for Final Angular Velocity
text: To find ω, take the square root of both sides:\nω $$=$$ $$\sqrt{3000}$$
